title: Bootstrap State
description: Schema for the Bootstrap State manifest: Cloud-Init snippets, base image registry, VM template registry, deployment provenance records, secret registry, DNS registry, service contracts, hardware requirements, and first-boot ordering.

cell_id.description: Stable unique identity of the Infrastructure Cell this bootstrap state belongs to

declared_at.description: Timestamp when this bootstrap state was last updated

external_backup.description: External backup configuration — where configuration state and docs are mirrored outside the cell. Set null provider to skip external backup.

host_identity.description: Proxmox host identity — discoverable by Tier 1 assessment or init-bootstrap-state.py

vm_defaults.description: Default values applied to all VMs unless overridden per-VM. User input at cell initialization.

storage_config.description: Proxmox storage identifiers — discoverable by Tier 1 assessment (pvesm status)

keepass_config.description: KeePass organizational conventions — user input at cell initialization

vms.description: Per-VM bootstrap manifest — one entry per managed VM

base_images.description: ISO and base image registry — authoritative source of truth for base images

templates.description: VM template registry — one entry per named Proxmox template

provenance_records.description: Deployment provenance records — one record per VM deployment event

secrets.description: Secret reference registry — KeePass paths only, never secret values

dns_registry.description: Authoritative hostname-to-IP registry for all managed VMs and services

service_contracts.description: Service contract declarations — interfaces provided and required per service

external_dependencies.description: Declared external dependencies — services outside the cell boundary (DNS providers, SMTP relays, certificate authorities, etc.)

network_topology_declared.description: Declared bridge, VLAN, and firewall topology (Phase 8). Source of truth for network reconstruction and drift detection. Distinct from network_topology (Cloud-Init CIDR/gateway parameters).

capacity_model.description: Declared capacity thresholds and observed utilization snapshot (Phase 11). Used by readiness scorer to surface capacity risk and verify restoration headroom.

backup_config.description: Restic + rclone backup configuration (Phase 6.B). Three layers: secrets (KeePass DB, rclone copy — no restic), config (bootstrap state + assessment, encrypted restic), appdata (VM data volumes, encrypted restic, opt-in). Distinct from external_backup (git mirroring).

network_topology.description: Management network topology — single source of truth for gateway, subnet, nameservers, and interface. All network-config snippets are generated from this.

hardware_requirements.description: BIOS and firmware requirements that must be met before Proxmox installation

first_boot_order.description: Ordered list of VM names specifying first-boot provisioning sequence

name.description: VM name as configured in Proxmox

role.description: Functional role of this VM, e.g. forgejo, inventory, assessment-engine

template_name.description: Name of the Proxmox VM template this VM is cloned from

initial_ip.description: Static IP address assigned by Cloud-Init network-config on first boot

initial_hostname.description: Hostname set by Cloud-Init user-data on first boot

bridge.description: Proxmox bridge the VM's primary NIC is attached to, e.g. vmbr0

initial_user.description: OS username created by Cloud-Init, e.g. ubuntu

ssh_key_reference.description: Secret Registry ID for the SSH public key placed by Cloud-Init

password_reference.description: Secret Registry ID for the initial OS user password

extra_packages.description: Role-specific packages added to the base package list for this VM's user-data snippet. Used by generate-user-data.py.

workspace_path.description: Override workspace directory for this VM's runcmd. If null, uses vm_defaults.workspace_base_path + VM name.

containers.description: Containerized applications running on this VM. Populated during init and updated by Tier 2 assessment.

container_declaration.description: Declared configuration for a containerized application. Records everything needed to deterministically re-setup the container on a restored system.

name.description: Container name, e.g. postgresql or forgejo-app

runtime.description: Container runtime used to manage this container

image.description: Full image reference including registry, e.g. registry.hub.docker.com/library/postgres

image_tag.description: Image tag, informational only. Tags can move; the digest is the stable reference.

image_digest.description: SHA256 digest of the image, e.g. sha256:abc123... Pinning this guarantees the exact image bytes regardless of tag reassignment. Populated by Tier 2 assessment at deployment time.

compose_file.description: Reference to the compose or Quadlet file in Forgejo. The file content is not stored here — only the reference and hash for verification.

ports.description: Port bindings declared for this container

volumes.description: Volume mount declarations for this container

data_volumes.description: Subset of volumes that contain persistent data and require backup. Preferred method is restic (managed by backup_config, Phase 6.B). Legacy methods: PBS full-VM backup, rclone, encrypted-archive.

alias.description: Hostname alias for this container inside the network

env_vars.description: Non-secret environment variables. Key-value pairs stored in plain text. Secret values go in secret_references instead.

secret_references.description: Secret Registry IDs for environment variables that contain secrets

minimum_capability.description: Minimum hardware resources this container requires to run

startup_after.description: Names of other containers or services that must be running before this container starts

health_check.description: Command to verify the container is healthy after start, e.g. 'curl -sf http://localhost:3000/api/healthz'

compose_file_reference.description: Reference to a compose or Quadlet file stored in Forgejo. The file content is version-controlled in Forgejo; only the reference is stored here.

file_path.description: Path within the repo, e.g. containers/forgejo/compose.yaml

commit_hash.description: Git commit hash at time of last deployment

file_digest.description: SHA256 of the file content at time of last deployment, for change detection

host_path.description: Absolute path on the VM host filesystem

options.description: Mount options, e.g. ro (read-only) or Z (SELinux relabel)

volume_name.description: Logical name for this volume, used in backup archive filenames

data_volume.description: A volume that contains persistent data and requires external backup. Corresponds to a volume_mount entry.

volume_name.description: Logical name used in backup archive filenames, e.g. forgejo-repos or postgresql-data

host_path.description: Absolute path on the VM host filesystem where this data lives

backup_method.description: How this volume is backed up. restic: preferred method — per-volume restic repo with per-backup unique key, managed by backup_config (Phase 6.B). pbs: covered by full VM PBS backup. rclone: legacy per-volume rclone to remote. encrypted-archive: legacy per-volume GPG archive.

backup_schedule.description: Cron expression for backup schedule if backup_method is restic, rclone, or encrypted-archive. For PBS, schedule is managed by PBS itself. For restic, schedule is also managed by backup_config but declared here for per-volume overrides.

backup_destination.description: Destination for restic, rclone, or encrypted-archive backups. For restic, this is the restic_repo_root prefix; full path assembled as {root}/{cell_id}/appdata/{component}/. Not used for PBS.

retention_count.description: Number of snapshots or archives to retain. For restic, overrides the backup_config layer default for this volume. For rclone/encrypted-archive: number of archive files retained.

pbs_job.description: PBS backup job ID if backup_method is pbs, e.g. pbs-daily-vms

estimated_size_gb.description: Estimated current size of this volume in GB (informational, updated by Tier 2 assessment)

container_minimum_capability.description: Minimum hardware resources required to run this container. Used by the compatibility checker to validate the restored system has sufficient capacity.

ram_mb.description: Minimum RAM in MB required for this container

disk_gb.description: Minimum free disk space in GB required on the volume host path

user_data_path.description: Repository-relative path to the Cloud-Init user-data snippet

user_data_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of user-data content at time of last deployment

network_config_path.description: Repository-relative path to the Cloud-Init network-config snippet

network_config_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of network-config content at time of last deployment

vendor_data_path.description: Repository-relative path to the Proxmox vendor-data snippet, if used

source_url.description: Canonical download URL for the ISO

checksum.description: SHA-256 checksum of the ISO file, prefixed with sha256:

created_at.description: Timestamp when the Proxmox template was created from this ISO

included_packages.description: Packages pre-installed in the template beyond the base ISO

base_image.description: Name of the base image this template was built from

proxmox_template_id.description: Proxmox VM ID of the template, if stored on this host

additional_packages.description: Packages added to this template beyond the base image

tofu_workspace.description: OpenTofu workspace name used for this deployment

tofu_commit.description: Git commit hash of the OpenTofu repository at deployment time

template_checksum.description: SHA-256 checksum of the template used

ansible_commit.description: Git commit hash of the Ansible repository at deployment time

ansible_inventory_commit.description: Git commit hash of the Ansible inventory repository at deployment time

deployed_by.description: Operator identifier who performed the deployment

id.description: Stable unique identifier for this secret reference, e.g. pve01-root-password

description.description: Human-readable description of what this secret is

keepass_path.description: Full path within KeePass to the entry, e.g. Infrastructure/proxmox/pve01-root

owning_cell.description: cell_id of the Infrastructure Cell whose KeePass database holds this secret

required_by.description: Component identifiers that require this secret, e.g. host:pve01 or vm:forgejo

required_for.description: Operations enabled by this secret, e.g. ssh-access, ansible-execution

rotation_schedule.description: Human-readable rotation schedule, e.g. annually or on-compromise

recovery_path.description: How to obtain this secret if KeePass is inaccessible during recovery

hostname.description: FQDN or short hostname, e.g. forgejo.internal

vmid.description: Proxmox VM ID if this entry corresponds to a VM

role.description: Functional role, e.g. proxmox-host, forgejo, dns-server

provided_interfaces.description: Network interfaces exposed by this service

required_interfaces.description: Interfaces this service depends on from other services

startup_after.description: Service identifiers that must be running before this service starts

backup_job.description: PBS backup job identifier covering this service

secret_references.description: Secret Registry IDs required by this service

owner.description: Team or operator responsible for this service

protocol.description: Protocol name, e.g. https, postgresql, smtp

url_pattern.description: URL pattern for the interface, e.g. https://forgejo.internal

health_check.description: Health check method and path, e.g. GET /api/healthz

critical.description: If true, this service cannot start without this dependency

external_backup.description: Git repository mirroring configuration — mirrors configuration state and self-description data (bootstrap-state, snippets, assessment history, generated docs) to an external git host or encrypted archive. This is distinct from backup_config (Phase 6.B), which handles binary data backup (VMs, volumes, KeePass DB) via restic + rclone. external_backup answers 'how do I retrieve bootstrap state from scratch'; backup_config answers 'how do I restore VM data after failure'.

provider.description: Backup provider. github: mirror repos to private GitHub. encrypted-archive: produce timestamped encrypted tar.gz archives. null: no external backup configured.

github.description: GitHub-specific configuration (see backup_github_config definition). Required when provider is 'github'.

encrypted_archive.description: Encrypted archive configuration (see backup_archive_config definition). Required when provider is 'encrypted-archive'.

what_is_backed_up.description: Human-readable description of what is included in external backup. Auto-populated by setup-external-backup.py.

repos.description: Map of repo purpose to GitHub repo URL.

infrastructure.description: GitHub repo URL for OpenTofu / infrastructure-as-code

bootstrap.description: GitHub repo URL for bootstrap-state, snippets, registries

configuration.description: GitHub repo URL for Ansible inventory and playbooks

docs.description: GitHub repo URL for generated documentation

assessment_history.description: GitHub repo URL for assessment snapshots and history

deploy_key_reference.description: Secret Registry ID for the SSH private key used to push to GitHub

github_username.description: GitHub username or organisation owning the repos

backup_archive_config.description: Configuration for encrypted tar.gz archive backup.

destination.description: Where archives are stored. rclone remote path (e.g. gdrive:/backups/cell-id), local path, or scp target.

destination_type.description: How to transfer archives to the destination.

passphrase_reference.description: Secret Registry ID for the GPG symmetric encryption passphrase.

schedule.description: Cron expression or human-readable schedule for automated backups, e.g. '0 2 * * *' (daily at 02:00).

retention_count.description: Number of archives to retain. Older archives are removed automatically. null = keep all.

filename_prefix.description: Optional prefix prepended to archive filenames before cell_id. Normally omitted.

host_identity.description: Identity and role of the Proxmox host. Discoverable by Tier 1 assessment.

hostname.description: Short hostname of the Proxmox host, e.g. pve01. Discovered by: hostname(1)

fqdn.description: Fully-qualified domain name, e.g. pve01.internal. Discovered by: hostname --fqdn

proxmox_version.description: Proxmox VE version string. Discovered by: pveversion

domain.description: Operator's domain name, e.g. home.example.com. Set during forge phase-01 (plan). All node FQDNs use this as their suffix.

vm_defaults.description: Default values applied to all VMs. Timezone and workspace path require user input at init; initial_user is the OS image default.

timezone.description: Timezone for all VMs, e.g. Australia/Melbourne. Discoverable from host: timedatectl or /etc/timezone. User may override.

initial_user.description: Default OS username created by Cloud-Init. Ubuntu cloud images use 'ubuntu'. Override per-VM in vms[].initial_user.

workspace_base_path.description: Base directory for infrastructure workspaces created by Cloud-Init runcmd, e.g. /opt. Set null to skip workspace creation.

storage_config.description: Proxmox storage pool identifiers. Discoverable by Tier 1 assessment via pvesm status.

snippets.description: Proxmox storage path for Cloud-Init snippets, e.g. local:snippets. Discovered: pvesm status (storage with snippets content type)

isos.description: Proxmox storage path for ISOs, e.g. local:iso. Discovered: pvesm status (storage with iso content type)

vm_disks.description: Proxmox storage pool for VM disks, e.g. local-zfs or rpool. Discovered: pvesm status (storage with images content type)

keepass_config.description: KeePass organizational conventions. User input at cell initialization — reflects the operator's KeePass database structure.

root_path.description: Top-level KeePass group path used as prefix for all secrets in this cell, e.g. Infrastructure. All keepass_path values in the secret registry are relative to this root.

database_hint.description: Human-readable hint about which KeePass database file holds this cell's secrets, e.g. homelab.kdbx

network_topology.description: Management network topology. Authoritative source for all network-config snippet generation. Changing any field here requires re-running generate-network-configs.py.

management_cidr.description: CIDR of the management network, e.g. 192.168.50.0/24. The prefix length is extracted for use in network-config snippets.

gateway.description: Default IPv4 gateway for all VMs on the management network, e.g. 192.168.50.1

nameservers.description: Ordered list of DNS server IPs injected into all network-config snippets

interface_name.description: NIC interface name inside the VM, e.g. ens18 for VirtIO in Proxmox. All network-config snippets are generated for this interface.

headscale_url.description: Headscale coordination server URL, e.g. https://hatchery.home.example.com:8080. Used in spawn packages for WAN-mode broodling registration.

ddns_provider.description: External DNS auto-update provider: cloudflare | duckdns | lexicon:{provider-name} | null (static IP or manual)

ddns_zone.description: DNS zone being managed, e.g. home.example.com or duckdns.org

ddns_record.description: The A record name being auto-updated, e.g. hatchery or myhatchery (for duckdns.org)

ddns_credential_reference.description: KeePass path for the DNS provider API token/key, e.g. Infrastructure/cloudflare/api-token

ssl_provider.description: Let's Encrypt client used for host-level TLS certificates

ssl_method.description: ACME challenge method: dns-01-cloudflare | dns-01-duckdns | dns-01-acme.sh-cloudflare

ssl_cert_path.description: Path to the TLS fullchain certificate file on the Proxmox host

ssl_key_path.description: Path to the TLS private key file on the Proxmox host

hardware_requirements.description: BIOS and firmware requirements that must be verified before Proxmox installation

vtx_required.description: Intel VT-x or AMD-V hardware virtualization must be enabled

iommu_required.description: IOMMU must be enabled for PCI passthrough

minimum_ram_gb.description: Minimum RAM in GB required for this deployment

minimum_cores.description: Minimum physical CPU core count required

minimum_storage_gb.description: Minimum total storage in GB required

external_dependency.description: A declared external service dependency — outside the cell boundary

id.description: Stable unique identifier, e.g. cloudflare-dns, smtp-relay-sendgrid

certificate.description: TLS certificate state for HTTPS endpoints

capacity_model.description: Capacity thresholds and observed utilization for the Proxmox host.

thresholds.description: Operator-declared utilization thresholds. Utilization above these triggers gaps.

restoration_headroom_pct.description: Minimum free RAM % required to host all declared VMs after recovery (default 10)

vm_ram_total_gb.description: Sum of RAM allocated to all running VMs

trend.description: Trend data derived from historical snapshots (populated by capacity_collector.py).

days_to_ram_full.description: Estimated days until RAM is fully utilized at current trend rate

days_to_storage_full.description: Estimated days until storage is fully utilized

network_topology_declared.description: Declared network topology — bridges, VLANs, firewall policy. Source of truth for network reconstruction commands and drift scoring.

ip.description: Host IP in CIDR notation, e.g. 192.168.1.10/24

observed_bridges.description: Bridge state observed by Tier 2 collector (compare with bridges[] to detect drift)

backup_config.description: Top-level backup configuration — layers, destinations, history.

checkpoint_tag.description: Restic snapshot tag for permanent checkpoints (default: checkpoint)

all_failed_policy.description: Actions taken when all destinations fail for a layer

backup_history.description: Log of backup runs (most recent first; trimmed to last 100 entries)

backup_layer_secrets.description: Backup layer for the KeePass database. Uses plain rclone copy (no restic — KeePass is already AES-256 encrypted). Transport credentials stored in forge-manifest.json, not KeePass.

destinations.description: Ordered destination chain — each attempted in sequence; failures exposed and chain continues

backup_layer_restic.description: Backup layer using restic (config state or appdata). All repos encrypted; per-backup unique key stored in KeePass.

destinations.description: Ordered destination chain — each attempted in sequence

backup_destination_secrets.description: A rclone destination for the KeePass database backup.

backup_destination_restic.description: A restic destination for config or appdata backup.

restic_repo_root.description: Root path; full repo path assembled as {root}/{cell_id}/{layer}/{component}/

restic_repo_password_keepass_prefix.description: KeePass path prefix; full key path: {prefix}/{component}/{timestamp}/repo-password

backup_run_record.description: Record of a single backup run.

backup_component_record.description: Per-component result within a backup run.
